Are you ready to tackle real-world problems and make an immediate impact?


Join BenchSci for a 4-month contract internship and contribute directly to our mission of
accelerating drug discovery. You'll work alongside experienced engineers, delivering solutions for features on small to medium-sized scope and complexity. This is a chance to acquire higher-level skills while making a tangible difference.  

Pay range: $25 - $35 per hour


What you’ll be doing (Scope & Accountability):


● Delivery: Be accountable for delivering solutions for features, typically handling one or
several small tasks up to a medium complexity story.
● Problem Solving: Identify problems and relevant issues in straightforward situations. You
will gather facts and generate possible solutions.
● Team Contribution: Actively contribute to your team, share experiences, and exchange ideas in a collaborative environment.
● Growth: Demonstrate competence in your core area while actively acquiring new skills.
How you’ll work:
● Execution: Contribute to the execution of specific tasks within your team.
● Guidance: Work with minimal instructions on routine tasks and general instructions on new assignments.
● Clarity: Learn to recognize when information is missing to make a decision and proactively seek clarification from mentors and teammates.


Qualifications (What we're looking for):

● Currently enrolled in a degree program in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field.
● A strong foundation in programming and software development principles.
● Eagerness to learn, adapt, and seek clarification when needed.
● Excellent collaboration and communication skills.

Contract Details:
● Duration: 4 Months (Short-term contract)
● Location: College Street, Toronto
● Start Date: On or before May 1, 2026  


Ready to start your journey in tech and drive meaningful change in science? Apply today!

About BenchSci:

BenchSci's mission is to exponentially increase the speed and quality of life-saving research and development. We empower scientists to run more successful experiments with the world's most advanced, biomedical artificial intelligence software platform.   

Backed by Generation Investment Management, TCV, Inovia, F-Prime, Golden Ventures, and Google's AI fund, Gradient Ventures, we provide an indispensable tool for scientists that accelerates research at top pharmaceutical companies and leading academic centers.  

Our Culture:

Our culture fosters transparency, collaboration, and continuous learning.   

We value each other's differences and always look for opportunities to embed equity into the fabric of our work. We foster diversity, autonomy, and personal growth, and provide resources to support motivated self-leaders in continuous improvement.   

You will work with high-impact, highly skilled, and intelligent experts motivated to drive impact and fulfill a meaningful mission. We empower you to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and thrive. Here you will be challenged to stretch yourself to achieve the seemingly impossible.   

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion: 

We're committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We believe that improving diversity, equity and inclusion is our collective responsibility, and this belief guides our DEI journey. 

Learn more about our DEI initiatives.  

Accessibility Accommodations: 

Should you require any accommodation, we will work with you to meet your needs. Please reach out to talent@benchsci.com.

************************************************************************* 

About Rivian



Rivian is on a mission to keep the world adventurous forever. This goes for the emissions-free Electric Adventure Vehicles we build, and the curious, courageous souls we seek to attract. 

 

As a company, we constantly challenge what’s possible, never simply accepting what has always been done. We reframe old problems, seek new solutions and operate comfortably in areas that are unknown. Our backgrounds are diverse, but our team shares a love of the outdoors and a desire to protect it for future generations. 


Role Summary

Internship Term: Summer 2026 This is a bucket application, and is not an official opening. Your application can be considered for Rivian’s 2026 internship programs. If you are selected, you will receive communication with specific responsibilities and qualifications for the role you are being considered for.

 

Rivian internships are experiences optimized for student candidates. To be eligible, you must be an undergraduate or graduate student in an accredited program during the internship term with an expected graduation date between Fall 2026 through Spring 2028. Rivian's Internship Program requires active student enrollment. Information regarding your expected degree completion date is collected solely to verify eligibility and determine your availability for future full-time opportunities. Rivian is an equal opportunity employer and does not use graduation dates to determine the age of applicants or as a basis for discriminatory hiring decisions.  

 

If you are not pursuing a degree, please see our full time positions on our Rivian careers site. 

 

Note that if your university has specific requirements for internship programs, it is your responsibility to fulfill those requirements. 


Responsibilities



Applying to this opening will place you into consideration for Rivian’s 2026 summer internship program.
Teams your application can be considered for include:
Autonomy
ADAS
Electrical Hardware, Sensors


Qualifications



Must be currently pursuing a bachelors, masters, or PhD degree at an accredited university.
Actively pursuing a degree or one closely related in computer science, computer engineering, electrical engineering or similar.
Development experience in one or more areas of computer vision, image/video, deep learning.
Experience programming in C++, Python, or related languages.
Solid understanding of machine learning, deep learning, and neural network architectures.
Proficiency in using computer vision and deep learning toolkits.
Passionately motivated to take ideas from R&D phase to a product
Strong written communication and presentation skills.

 


Pay Disclosure



The salary range for this role is $33.00-51.00/HR for Palo Alto based applicants, $26.00-45.00/HR for Irvine based applicants, and $25.00-$40.00/HR for Normal or Plymouth based applicants. This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. An employee’s position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, specific competencies, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, shift, and organizational needs.

 

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits including but not limited to paid vacation, paid sick leave, and medical insurance benefits. More information about benefits is available at rivianbenefits.com. 

 

You can apply for this role through careers.rivian.com (or through internal-careers-rivian.icims.com if you are a current employee). There is no fixed deadline for this application; applications are accepted on an ongoing basis until the role is filled or the opening is no longer needed.

 

Internship positions are not eligible for retirement benefits.

Why Meridian?

Factor us into your next career opportunity. We want you to grow with us and have an experience that’s different. This is a place where you can expect the unexpected. 

Find our story here: About Meridian



The role: 4-Month Term (Co-Op/Internship)

The Data and Artificial Intelligence Student has a dynamic role responsible for delivering data-driven insights to Meridian’s departments. This position involves a wide range of data tasks including data analysis, business intelligence, data science, and data engineering.

Responsibilities:

Assist in building and evaluating predictive models and statistical analyses
Assist in designing, testing, and refining generative AI agents for internal data workflows

title: Co-Op, Manufacturing Sciences & Technology SU26
company: BlueRock Therapeutics LP - Toronto, Ontario, Canada
full_description:
HOW TO APPLY: 

Step 1: 

Apply ONLINE ASAP at LINK:

https://jobs.lever.co/bluerocktx/755a331c-5858-4ceb-807b-55a783d50a63

 

Step 2: 

Once you have applied ONLINE directly to the employer, you MUST also click the APPLY button in CAREER CONNECTIONS. You are required to upload a RESUME ONLY in Career Connections. BOTH steps are required for this position.   

 

************************************************************************* 

Who is BlueRock?


BlueRock Therapeutics LP is a clinical stage cell therapy harnessing the power of cell therapy to create a pipeline of new medicines for people suffering from neurological and ophthalmic diseases. Two of our novel investigational cell therapies, bemdaneprocel (BRT-DA01) for the treatment of Parkinson’s disease and OpCT-001 for the treatment of primary photoreceptor diseases are clinical stage programs. BlueRock was founded in 2016 as a joint venture of Versant Ventures and Leaps by Bayer. Our culture is defined by the courage to persist regardless of the challenge, the urgency to transform medicine and deliver hope, integrity guided by mission, and community-mindedness with the understanding that we are all part of something bigger than ourselves.


What Are We Doing?


Our foundational science harnesses the ability to create and then direct the differentiation of universal pluripotent stem cells into authentic, functional cells that can be used as allogeneic cellular therapies to treat a broad array of diseases. We can also further engineer these cells, enabling them to produce enzymes, antibodies, and other proteins for additional therapeutic benefit. We are passionate about delivering on the promise of cellular and gene therapy, shaping the future of cellular medicine, and delivering new therapies to millions of patients with treatment options. We are seeking individuals who are collaborative, thrive in a fun and dynamic culture, and are steadfast in the commitment to advance cutting-edge cellular therapies to impact patients’ lives.

The Manufacturing Sciences & Technology Co-Op will support technology transfers, process monitoring, raw materials lifecycle management, asset management, and GMP manufacturing to ensure the creation of protocols and processes that incorporate contemporary technologies and methodologies and provide products that are consistent with scientific and clinical goals of the program. Additionally, the co-op will draft and review data systems, protocols and reports, and provide technical assessments for process changes and documentation pertaining to process development, characterization, and validation to meet regulatory requirements. The successful candidate will act as a resource for the Manufacturing Sciences & Technology group, and will collaborate with other groups such as process development, analytical development, facilities, and manufacturing including internal and external development partners.

Responsibilities

Support all Tech Transfer activities related to process transfer from Process Development to the manufacturing,  such as writing or reviewing technical documentation (tech transfer plans, raw material and equipment assessments, protocols, reports, or procedures). 
Interpret manufacturing process data, identify trends, troubleshoot problems, and drive data-driven decision-making with effective and concise communications.
Assist in developing and implementing strategies for continuous improvement across manufacturing processes that reduce cost, save time, improve process attributes, and optimize quality.
Interface with Analytical & Quality Control and Quality Assurance teams to ensure compliant tech transfer activities are ongoing.

Qualifications & Requirements

Currently enrolled or recently graduated in a Bachelor’s or graduate degree program at an accredited college or university in Engineering or related field of study.
Ability to work a 6-month assignment, full-time.
Must be 18 years of age by the start of the co-op.
Must have transportation to the site in Toronto, ON Canada
This position is intended to be on-site and candidates will be informed should anything change.
Excellent organizational skills, sufficient to multi-task in an extremely fast-paced environment with changing priorities.
Ability to work independently, have excellent communication skills, and ability to influence across multiple functions.

$30 - $30 an hour

Job Description: 

Junior IT Support / DevOps Assistant

About the Role
We’re looking for a junior team member who will support day‑to‑day IT operations, help desk activities, and basic systems administration. You’ll work closely with senior staff to support Windows and Linux servers, Active Directory, user support, and general troubleshooting across the organization.

Compensation & Role Details:

Expected Salary Range: The base hourly rate for this role is expected to be between $20–$23 CAD, depending on experience and qualifications.

Role Type: New Role

AI Disclosure: AI may be used during the hiring process.

Key Responsibilities

Help Desk & User Support

Provide first‑level technical support to staff via email, phone, or in person.

Troubleshoot issues with desktops, laptops, peripherals, software, and basic network problems.

Assist users with password resets, access issues, and general IT inquiries.

Maintain accurate documentation of tickets, fixes, and procedures.

Support onboarding and offboarding processes (hardware setup, account creation, permissions).

Active Directory & Account Management

Assist with permissions, security groups, and organizational unit tasks.

Windows & Linux Systems Support

Help escalate system issues and collect diagnostic information for senior engineers.

Other Responsibilities

Perform hardware setup (workstations, monitors, peripherals).

Assist with software installation and configuration.

Support inventory tracking of IT equipment.

Help with simple scripting or automation tasks if comfortable (PowerShell/Bash).

Observe and follow documented runbooks and escalation procedures.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Basic understanding of Windows desktop and server environments.

Familiarity with Linux fundamentals (navigation, basic commands).

Introductory knowledge of Active Directory concepts (users, groups, OU structure).

Good troubleshooting and problem‑solving skills.

Strong communication skills and willingness to assist others patiently.

Ability to follow instructions and document work clearly.

Interest in progressing toward systems administration or DevOps in the future.

************************************************************************* 

OUR STORY
TechInsights is the information Platform for the semiconductor industry.

Regarded as the most trusted source of actionable, in-depth intelligence related to semiconductor innovation and surrounding markets, TechInsights’ content informs decision makers and professionals whose success depends on accurate knowledge of the semiconductor industry—past, present, or future.

Over 650 companies and 150,000 users access the TechInsights Platform, the world’s largest vertically integrated collection of unmatched reverse engineering, teardown, and market analysis in the semiconductor industry. This collection includes detailed circuit analysis, imagery, semiconductor process flows, device teardowns, illustrations, costing and pricing information, forecasts, market analysis, and expert commentary. TechInsights’ customers include the most successful technology companies who rely on TechInsights’ analysis to make informed business, design, and product decisions faster and with greater confidence. For more information, visit www.techinsights.com.


WHY WORK WITH US

Company-sponsored training and development opportunities
Community involvement opportunities through charitable alliances: https://www.techinsights.com/community-involvement
Wellness resources and support
Inclusive environment that prioritizes diversity, equity, and accessibility
High-growth company driven by high performance
Expected pay range: CAD $20 - $25/Hour

THE OPPORTUNITY: 
TechInsights is looking for a motivated Junior Data Analyst to join our team for a 4-month co-op. In this role, you will support semiconductor product and process technology initiatives by collecting, organizing, and analyzing primary data from lab analyses, technical reports, and experimental results. You will work directly with a Senior Technical Fellow and contribute to data-driven insights that inform our research and product teams.
 
WHAT YOU’LL DO 

Collect, measure, and extract data from semiconductor analysis labs (e.g., SEM, TEM) and technical reports published in the TechInsights Platform.
Organize and document data in structured formats, including Excel tables, graphs, and charts.
Analyze and interpret experimental results to support data-driven insights.
Prepare reports and presentations using Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
Assist in developing and applying data for product and process improvement initiatives.
Collaborate with technical staff to ensure accurate and actionable data documentation.

 
WHAT YOU’LL BRING 

Current enrollment in a post secondary program in the fields on Data Analysis, Data Science, Statistics, or related fields.
Basic knowledge of semiconductor products, process technologies, or materials science is an asset.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office, with solid data visualization skills in Excel.
Strong attention to detail and the ability to work accurately across multiple data sources.
Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret technical data and produce meaningful outputs.
Clear communication skills and a collaborative approach.
Eagerness to take ownership of assigned tasks and grow through hands-on experience.


As part of the recruitment process for this position, you will be required to submit your latest citizenship and/or permanent residency information. This information will be used to comply with U.S. Export Control Laws and Regulations.

WORKING ARRANGEMENT
This role is on-site at our Ottawa office.
 

Technology knows no bounds, and neither does TechInsights. Bringing together talented humans from different perspectives, backgrounds and abilities is something we take seriously. We’re committed to building an inclusive environment that welcomes you to be your authentic self and allows us to push past the boundaries together.

TechInsights is committed to meeting the needs of people with disabilities. Accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.

As part of any recruitment process, TechInsights collects and processes personal data relating to job applicants. We are committed to being transparent about how we collect and use that data and to meeting our data protection obligations. Our Privacy policy can be referenced here: https://www.techinsights.com/privacy-policy

Availability: *Full-time availability of 37.5 – 40 hours weekly is required to be eligible for this opportunity.

Experience working for one of the fastest growing Human Capital Management technology companies in the world Access to Dayforce’s development programs and resources

Autonomy to bring forth new ideas and optimize existing structures within the organization

About the opportunity:

We are looking for a Marketing Automation Intern to join the Marketing Automation team within the Dayforce Marketing department. This role will be to work closely with the Marketing Automation Partner, Project and Traffic Management, and Growth Marketing teams for campaign execution.

 

Each intern will report to the Manager, Marketing Automation and be responsible for tasks including, developing and maintain automation programs, ensuring accurate and timely execution of program activities, reviewing and testing (QA) programs created by other team members, maintaining quality of marketing data, and assisting with creation of documentation and analytical reports. Assist with the creation and analysis of campaign performance reporting to inform and guide our marketing partners

Process and detail-oriented.
Organized and able to work within time constraints and due dates.

Excellent communication skills and confident presence
Excellent interpersonal skills to ensure successful interactions and strong working relationships across marketing team.

Working towards Bachelor’s degree. Preferably in Business, Marketing, or related field.

Knowledge of Microsoft Office applications: Outlook, Word, Excel, Teams

Knowledge of CRMs/Reporting tools: Salesforce and PowerBI

Summary:

This role will work closely with Data Scientists on the Data Insights team at Clio with a high level of mentorship and technical guidance. Analytical decisions and modeling approaches will typically be made in collaboration with senior team members.

Role Expectations:

Work on Clio’s Data Insights team and gain exposure to how data science shapes product strategy, customer experience, and business decisions.

Partner with Data Scientists and cross-functional teams to contribute to Clio’s mission to transform the legal experience for all.

Support data-driven projects and lead smaller analyses independently. This work could include:

Assisting in exploratory data analysis to uncover product usage patterns, trends, or anomalies

Preparing datasets using Python and SQL for modeling, reporting, or dashboarding

Supporting the development and evaluation of machine learning models (e.g. churn prediction, user segmentation)

Running A/B test analyses and helping summarize experimental outcomes

Building and updating dashboards, slide decks, or notebooks to present findings

Maintaining project tracking tools like dbt, GitHub, or internal documentation

Participating in sprint ceremonies, code reviews, and team knowledge shares

Contributing to Clio’s internal data science playbooks, templates, and best practices

Supporting ad-hoc data requests or mini-projects from Product, Marketing, or Customer teams

You’ll be part of a collaborative team that values curiosity, continuous learning, and the ethical use of AI and data. You’ll leave this co-op with hands-on experience in applied data science and an understanding of how data drives business value at a high-growth SaaS company.

Required Technical Skills:

Ability to work collaboratively on a cross-functional team including Data Scientists, Engineers, Product Managers, and Designers

Analytical mindset (Critical thinking, curiosity, and a strong ability to break down complex problems using data)

Proficiency in Python and SQL for data exploration, analysis, and basic modeling

Comfortable using data notebooks (Jupyter, Colab) and version control tools (GitHub)

Familiar with spreadsheet tools and able to organize findings clearly in Excel or Google Sheets

Ability to document analysis clearly and communicate results through visualizations or slides

Bonus: Experience with A/B testing, basic machine learning libraries (e.g. pandas, scikit-learn, or XGBoost), or data storytelling tools (e.g. Looker Studio, Tableau, Seaborn)

Key Traits for Success:

This role requires a learning mindset and a genuine enthusiasm for experimenting with new tools, techniques, and problem domains.

This role calls for resourcefulness and initiative, especially when navigating ambiguous datasets or scoping your own analysis.

This role demands a keen eye for detail—whether it’s catching an unexpected outlier in a dataset, spotting inconsistencies in SQL logic, or flagging edge cases in a model’s output.

This role is human-centered and requires empathy for Clio’s customers, curiosity about the legal industry, and a strong sense of ethical responsibility when working with real-world data.

Job Requirements:

A 1st year Masters Data Science student or a fourth year undergrad student, enrolled in a co-op program

Focus on data science, statistics, machine learning

Available for an 8 month co-op term (May to December)

Based in Toronto, Vancouver, or Calgary

Description

Would you like to intern with one of the brightest Dev teams in North America? A tight-knit group of some of the best developers, problem-solvers, and software engineers around. They’re scary-smart. They love what they do. And they’re using their coding genius to make the world a better place. This isn’t just an internship —  if you've got the chops, it’s a launchpad for a lifetime of success. 

About Us

At AlgaeCal, we’re driven by a single idea. To end the fear of bone loss. It’s a big mission. In the United States, an estimated 54 million people have fragile bones. And they’re scared. Scared of snapping a bone. Scared of being forced to quit their favorite hobbies. And worst of all, they are scared of losing their independence. 

The good news? AlgaeCal is the world’s only clinically backed natural solution to this problem. We help thousands of people say goodbye to fear, fracture, and frailty. If that excites you, you’ll find no better place to continue your career than at AlgaeCal. 

About You

You have a solid grasp of front-end development fundamentals and can skillfully transform theoretical concepts into functional, user-friendly interfaces.

About Your Opportunity
Here are a few things you can expect to be involved in while at AlgaeCal.

Get Great at Dev: You’ll gain hands-on experience creating seamless, user-friendly web experiences. You’ll work on projects ranging from responsive WordPress blog features to custom website templates. With guidance from our seasoned dev team, you’ll master the art of balancing clean code with outstanding design.

Bring Designs to Life: You’ll collaborate closely with designers and content creators to transform creative visions into functional, dynamic web elements. From pixel-perfect layouts to interactive features, you’ll bridge the gap between ideas and execution.

Bug Whispering: You’ll learn valuable debugging skills by tackling real-world challenges. Whether it’s fixing quirky CSS issues or solving JavaScript puzzles, you’ll learn how to become the go-to problem solver on the team. In fact, you’ll get so good at squishing bugs we might even send you to Klendathu.

Master the Dev Arsenal: Sharpen your skills with the latest tools and frameworks. From Semantic HTML and Sass to React and Node.js, you’ll expand your knowledge base and grow your expertise in the tech stack we use every day.

Flex Your Feature-Building Muscle: You’ll learn how to develop and deploy features for our WordPress blog and website templates. This is your chance to own projects and showcase your ability to deliver impactful solutions that look great and work even better.

Collaboration & Communication: Be part of a tight-knit team where your ideas matter. You’ll participate in brainstorming sessions, sprint planning, and code reviews, growing both your technical and collaborative skills in a supportive environment.

Stay Ahead of the Curve: Get hands-on experience working with cutting-edge technologies and platforms, like AWS and Bitbucket. You’ll learn to navigate these tools like a pro while staying in sync with the latest industry trends.

What Will You Earn? 

Our Developer Intern position offers $3500 monthly.

You’ll also enjoy the following benefits:

Monthly team events and activities
Weekly team lunches
Casual dress code
Incredible dog friendly, Yaletown office one block from SkyTrain

And above all, you’ll join a team that you’ll never want to leave. Culture is everything to us. Yes, we work extremely hard to change as many people’s lives as possible. But we know how to have fun and reward ourselves too.
